hypothesis: If a reinforcement learning molecular generation framework incorporates a dual-objective reward function that simultaneously maximizes pharmacophoric similarity (via CATS descriptors) to known estrogen receptor antagonists/degraders while minimizing structural similarity (via MACCS keys), then it will generate novel drug-like molecules with superior docking scores and drug-likeness compared to a baseline docking-only approach, because pharmacophore-guided optimization captures the essential binding features required for biological activity while the structural dissimilarity constraint drives exploration of chemically novel scaffolds beyond known chemical space.
